title: problem creating Debian based machine and issues with win-sshproxy.exe
body:
/kind bug

/kind feature

**Description**

I'm trying to create a machine using 



1. podman.exe machine init --image-path https://cloud.debian.org/images/cloud/bullseye/latest/debian-11-generic-amd64.qcow2 podman-debian11



**Describe the results you received:**

Downloading VM image: debian-11-generic-amd64.qcow2: done
Importing operating system into WSL (this may take 5+ minutes on a new WSL install)...
Import in progress, this may take a few minutes.
Unspecified error

Error: WSL import of guest OS failed: exit status 4294967295

**Describe the results you expected:**

machine installed

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

I have tried several debian distributions with same result either specifying the URL or the path to the downloaded image

Installing the default machine (using fedora) worked fine apart from

       API forwarding for Docker API clients is not available due to the following startup failures.
        win-sshproxy.exe failed to start, exit code: 1 (see windows event logs)

I could not find the error in the windows logs some additional pointers on this would be useful also instructions on how to run win-sshproxy.exe after the machine has been started



**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Client:       Podman Engine
Version:      4.1.0
API Version:  4.1.0
Go Version:   go1.16.15
Built:        Fri May  6 09:13:30 2022
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
podman-4.1.0-1.fc35.x86_64
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md)**


Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

comments.body: You cannot just use any old qcow2.   You have to use one that is prepared specifically for the environment of machine (it has to have a ignition file, podman (at the same version as the windows client), among other things like network support already installed into it.
Are there instructions for generating a suitable qcow2 file somewhere?

As Brent mentions this option is not for a generic Linux distro, it's to allow for a customized or updated variant of the same distro podman machine is using (currently Fedora).  The Linux distribution is provisioned by podman machine based on certain expectations, modifying system services, installing packages, including a custom recent copr package of podman, and various configuration changes. All these things are distribution specific so supporting additional distributions would require an additional code path per distro, and a maintainer to sign up for the ongoing work maintaining it. 

If you do decide to create a customized variant, WSL needs a rootfs tarball instead of a qcow (that's why you see the error). 

As to to the win-sshproxy error.  Can you paste the event logs. The easiest way to get them is through powershell:

`Get-WinEvent -ProviderName '.NET Runtime' -Oldest | Format-table -Wrap`

Internally win-sshproxy is making an ssh connection, so one simple thing to check is can you do `podman machine ssh` to the system. If you are able to login we know that the expected key is working and the distro is running.

I have an existing distribution based on Debian that I am using for my current work and I would like to be able to run podman on that distribution is there documentation somewhere on the changes you mentioned above so that I can apply these changes. I was able to build and run podman from the github repo  but I would like to apply the windows integration that the windows releaase provides

When I run:

Get-WinEvent -ProviderName '.NET Runtime' -Oldest | Format-table -Wrap

I get:

Get-WinEvent: There is not an event provider on the localhost computer that matches ".NET Runtime".

Did you install via the msi installer?. If there is no logs that implies the executable might not be at the expected location. The zip files we have include just the podman.exe remote client. 

If you intend to just use the podman command you could not use podman machine and instead  install/manage the distro yourself and just use the windows client as described here:
https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/docs/tutorials/mac_win_client.md

If you want docker clients to work though you need API forwarding, which you could still do with this approach by manually  starting and stopping win-sshproxy.exe

You could try to manually create what podman machine init does, and the start and stop would work, but it would be brittle, an update might break something and you would have to sort out the difference.

I had installed it originally from the github repo since I was mirroring what I did on my Linux droplet on Digital Ocean. Then I did a second install using the msi installer. Are there docs for win-sshproxy.exe somewhere it does not appear to support a help option other than the dialog box which is a bit cryptic 
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/2222402/168873995-5c88b3b7-8059-4856-b733-84f3948bde3f.png)
where is the source code for this app? I will take a look at mac_win_client.md and the source for "podman machine init"

Ah ok, so it looks for win-sshyproxy.exe in the same dir as the podman.exe so I would double check the one being ran has it next to it. 

The way you use it manually is like this:
`win-ssproxy.exe my-proxy c:\path\To\A\TempDir npipe:////./pipe/docker_engine  ssh://[user]@localhost:[port]//run/[user]/1000/podman/podman.sock C:\path\to\your\sshkey`

Replace [user] with your user and [port] with the port you decide to run the ssh service on your distro, and update the drive paths to point to where you want them (the latter needs to point to your identify key)

The source code to the proxy is here:
https://github.com/containers/gvisor-tap-vsock/blob/main/cmd/win-sshproxy/main.go

The source to machine init is here:
https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/pkg/machine/wsl/machine.go
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
